name: Istanbul
localTips: Buy an Istanbulkart at any kiosk or metro station — it works on trams, metro, buses and public ferries, and is far cheaper than single tickets bought each ride.
Mosques are free to enter but close briefly to visitors during the five daily prayer times — plan around midday Friday prayers especially, and bring a headscarf and modest clothing.
Restaurants directly on Sultanahmet's main tourist strip near the Blue Mosque are priced for tour groups — walk into Kadıköy on the Asian side or backstreets of Karaköy for the same food far cheaper.
The public commuter ferries across the Bosphorus cost a fraction of a tourist cruise and give arguably better views — hop on any Kadıköy or Üsküdar-bound boat from Eminönü.
Bargaining is expected in the Grand Bazaar and Spice Bazaar but not in regular shops or restaurants — a friendly opening counter-offer is around half the first price quoted.

activities.title: Sultanahmet Square & Hippodrome
activities.description: The heart of the old city, once a Roman chariot-racing track, now framed by the Blue Mosque and Hagia Sophia with an Egyptian obelisk still standing at its centre.

activities.title: Blue Mosque interior
activities.description: Six minarets and over 20,000 handmade İznik tiles give the Sultan Ahmed Mosque its name — free to enter outside prayer times, with a separate visitor entrance for non-worshippers.

activities.title: Hagia Sophia general prayer hall
activities.description: Nearly 1,500 years old — a Byzantine cathedral turned mosque turned museum turned mosque again — the vast domed main hall is free to enter as a working mosque outside prayer times.

activities.title: Galata Bridge & Golden Horn
activities.description: Cross the double-decker bridge lined with fishermen dangling lines into the Golden Horn — restaurants fill the lower level, and sunset views back to the old city are free from either side.

activities.title: Grand Bazaar browsing
activities.description: One of the world's oldest and largest covered markets, over 4,000 shops under 61 vaulted streets selling carpets, lanterns, spices and gold — free to wander for hours.

activities.title: Spice Bazaar browsing
activities.description: A 17th-century L-shaped market glowing with pyramids of saffron, sumac and Turkish delight — smaller and more manageable than the Grand Bazaar but just as intense.

activities.title: Gülhane Park
activities.description: The former outer garden of Topkapı Palace, now a public park with tulip beds in spring and a terrace café overlooking the Bosphorus at its far end.

activities.title: İstiklal Avenue & nostalgic tram
activities.description: A 1.4km pedestrian boulevard packed with shops, cinemas and side-street bars, with a red 1920s tram rattling its length from Taksim Square down to Tünel.

activities.title: Galata district streets
activities.description: Steep cobbled lanes climbing toward the Galata Tower, lined with design shops, cafés and street art in what was once Istanbul's Genoese trading quarter.

activities.title: Balat's colourful houses
activities.description: A once-Jewish and Greek quarter on the Golden Horn, its steep streets lined with brightly painted 19th-century houses that have made it one of the city's most photographed corners.

activities.title: Ortaköy waterfront square
activities.description: A picturesque baroque mosque sits right on the Bosphorus shore beneath the first bridge — the square behind it fills with street-food stalls and weekend crowds.

activities.title: Süleymaniye Mosque & courtyard views
activities.description: The Ottoman empire's grandest imperial mosque, designed by master architect Sinan, with a peaceful courtyard and terrace views over the Golden Horn far from the Sultanahmet crowds.

activities.title: Çamlıca Hill panorama
activities.description: The Asian side's highest point, with tea gardens and a sweeping view over both continents, the Bosphorus bridges and the old city skyline in the distance.

activities.title: Kadıköy market streets
activities.description: The Asian side's bohemian, food-obsessed neighbourhood — a Tuesday market, vintage shops and a completely different, more local pace than the tourist-heavy old city.

activities.title: Emirgan Park tulip season
activities.description: Millions of tulips bloom across this Bosphorus-side park every April during the Istanbul Tulip Festival, planted in vast geometric patterns across the hillside lawns.

activities.title: Turkish tea in a çay bahçesi
activities.description: Neighbourhood tea gardens with backgammon-playing regulars and tulip-shaped glasses of black tea are where actual Istanbullus socialise, not the touristy cafés of Sultanahmet.
activities.insiderNote: Pierre Loti Hill's tea garden above Eyüp has one of the best Golden Horn views, reached by a short cable car most tourists skip.

activities.title: A neighbourhood hamam, not a tourist one
activities.description: Skip the famous historic hamams' inflated prices for a smaller local bathhouse in Kadıköy or Fatih, where regulars go weekly and the scrub-down is just as thorough.
activities.insiderNote: Ask your accommodation host for their own neighbourhood hamam rather than booking one of the tourist-marketed historic ones — prices can be a third of the cost.

activities.title: Turkish coffee & fortune telling
activities.description: Order a Turkish coffee, flip the cup upside down when finished, and let the grounds' patterns be read by a café's resident fortune teller — a genuine local pastime, not a gimmick.
activities.insiderNote: Karaköy's older coffeehouses still have regular fortune tellers on hand most afternoons — ask staff if one is in that day.

activities.title: Commuter ferry across the Bosphorus
activities.description: Any regular Şehir Hatları ferry between Eminönü and Kadıköy or Üsküdar gives essentially the same Bosphorus views as a tourist cruise, for a fraction of the price, with tea sold on board.
activities.insiderNote: Sit on the open back deck and buy a glass of çay from the onboard cart — it's the classic Istanbul commute, not just a tourist activity.

activities.title: Meyhane night in Kadıköy's fish market
activities.description: The narrow lanes around Kadıköy's fish market fill with meyhane taverns serving raki, mezze and fresh fish to a mostly local crowd, far from the tourist restaurants across the water.
activities.insiderNote: Order raki 'ile' (with) a plate of white cheese and melon — the classic Turkish pairing that cuts the drink's strong anise flavour.

activities.title: Balık ekmek fish sandwich by the water
activities.description: Fish sandwiches grilled on rocking boats moored at Eminönü and sold straight to the dock — a genuine working-class Istanbul institution since Ottoman times.
activities.insiderNote: Eat standing at the dockside tables rather than the sit-down stalls a few metres back — same sandwich, local price.

activities.title: Çukurcuma antique quarter
activities.description: A hillside pocket of antique shops and the Museum of Innocence, tucked below İstiklal Avenue — the kind of neighbourhood most tour groups never find time for.
activities.insiderNote: Orhan Pamuk's actual Museum of Innocence, built to match his novel object-for-object, is here and rarely crowded.

activities.title: Simit & tea street breakfast
activities.description: A sesame-crusted simit ring bought from a red street cart, eaten with a glass of tea on a curb or ferry dock, is how most Istanbullus actually start their day.
activities.insiderNote: Carts near ferry terminals sell it freshest in the early morning batch — avoid ones that have been sitting out since the previous evening.

activities.title: Topkapı Palace & Harem entry
activities.description: The Ottoman sultans' primary residence for 400 years — treasury jewels, sacred relics and the labyrinthine Harem quarters where the empire's real politics played out.
activities.bookingQuery: Topkapi Palace Harem entry ticket Istanbul

activities.title: Basilica Cistern entry
activities.description: A vast 6th-century underground reservoir held up by 336 columns, two of them resting on giant carved Medusa heads — cool, atmospheric and lit for dramatic effect.

activities.title: Bosphorus evening dinner cruise
activities.description: Sail between two continents past Ottoman waterfront mansions and the floodlit bridges, with a multi-course dinner and often live music on deck.

activities.title: Historic hamam spa experience
activities.description: A full traditional bath ritual — steam room, foam wash and scrub — inside a centuries-old bathhouse like Çemberlitaş or Süleymaniye Hamamı, built by imperial architects.

activities.title: Whirling dervishes Sema ceremony
activities.description: A solemn, hypnotic Sufi ritual of spinning dance performed to live music in a restored 17th-century lodge — a genuine spiritual tradition, not simply a tourist performance.
activities.bookingQuery: whirling dervishes Sema ceremony ticket Istanbul

activities.title: Dolmabahçe Palace tour
activities.description: A 19th-century Bosphorus-front palace dripping in crystal chandeliers and gold leaf, where the Ottoman sultans moved to feel more European — Atatürk died in this building.

activities.title: Guided Grand Bazaar shopping tour
activities.description: A local guide navigates the bazaar's 4,000 shops and teaches proper bargaining etiquette for carpets, ceramics and jewellery — worth it just to not get lost.
activities.bookingQuery: Grand Bazaar guided shopping tour Istanbul

activities.title: Turkish night: belly dance & folk show
activities.description: A high-energy evening of belly dancing, folk performances from across Anatolia and live music, usually paired with an unlimited local drinks package and dinner.
activities.bookingQuery: Turkish night belly dance show Istanbul

activities.title: Princes' Islands day trip
activities.description: A ferry ride to car-free Büyükada, where horse-drawn carriages and bicycles replace traffic, wooden Ottoman-era mansions line pine-shaded lanes, and seafood restaurants face the sea.
activities.bookingQuery: Princes Islands Buyukada day trip from Istanbul

activities.title: Chora Church (Kariye) mosaics
activities.description: A small Byzantine church covered floor to dome in the finest surviving mosaics and frescoes from the 14th century — far less crowded than Hagia Sophia, and arguably more beautiful.
activities.bookingQuery: Chora Church Kariye Museum entry ticket Istanbul
